title: Substack vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack and AWeber: pricing, features, and which email platform suits your needs.
intro: Substack and AWeber serve different audiences in the email marketing space. Substack focuses on writer monetization through paid subscriptions, while AWeber is a traditional email marketing platform for businesses and creators. Choosing between them depends on your primary goal: building a direct subscription business or managing customer communications.

comparison_table.tool_a: Notes feed, podcast hosting, audience discovery
comparison_table.tool_b: Autoresponders, landing pages, RSS-to-email, AMP emails

choose_a_if: You want to monetize a newsletter directly from readers with minimal platform fees
You're building a writer-focused publication and need unlimited subscribers on the free tier
You want built-in podcast hosting and audience discovery features
choose_b_if: You need comprehensive marketing automation and email campaign management tools
You want better customer support and higher user satisfaction ratings
You're a solopreneur or small business looking for affordable, proven email marketing functionality

faq.a: Neither is universally better—they solve different problems. Substack excels at writer monetization with unlimited free subscribers, while AWeber provides stronger email marketing features and automation. AWeber has better overall ratings on review sites.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or AWeber?
faq.a: Substack is free forever with no subscriber limits, but takes 10% of paid subscription revenue. AWeber's free tier supports 500 subscribers; paid plans start at $15/month. For small lists, Substack is cheaper; for larger audiences without paid subscriptions, AWeber may cost less.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your subscriber list. However, they serve different purposes—Substack is designed for monetization, while AWeber is for marketing automation. Migration depends on whether you're switching your entire strategy.
